heading.description: Headings use 1-6 hash symbols. # is H1 (largest), ###### is H6 (smallest).

paragraph.description: A paragraph is one or more consecutive lines of text, separated by blank lines.

emphasis.description: Single asterisks or underscores for italic, double for bold, triple for both.

link.description: Inline links contain the URL in parentheses. Optional title in quotes.

image.description: Images use the same syntax as links but prefixed with !.

code_inline.description: Inline code is wrapped in backticks. Use double backticks to include literal backticks.

code_block.description: Fenced code blocks use triple backticks with optional language identifier for syntax highlighting.

list_unordered.description: Unordered lists use -, *, or + as markers. Nested lists indent by 2-4 spaces.

list_ordered.description: Ordered lists use numbers followed by a period. The actual numbers don't matter for rendering.

blockquote.description: Blockquotes prefix lines with >. Can be nested with multiple > characters.

horizontal_rule.description: Three or more hyphens, asterisks, or underscores on a line create a horizontal rule.

table.description: Tables use pipes and hyphens. Alignment: :--- (left), :---: (center), ---: (right).

task_list.description: Task lists use - [ ] for unchecked and - [x] for checked items (GitHub Flavored Markdown).

strikethrough.description: Strikethrough text is wrapped in double tildes (GitHub Flavored Markdown).

footnote.description: Footnotes use [^id] for reference and [^id]: for the definition.

frontmatter.description: YAML frontmatter between --- delimiters provides metadata (title, date, tags) for static site generators.

html.description: Raw HTML can be embedded in Markdown. Block-level HTML must be separated by blank lines.

String.description: Text data type representing a sequence of characters.

Number.description: Numeric data type for integer and floating-point values.

Boolean.description: Logical type with two values: true and false.

Array.description: Ordered collection of elements accessible by index.

Object.description: Collection of key-value pairs (properties and methods).
